content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 16, 1992

Mr. James Sutton
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor
591 Redwood Highway, #4000
Mill Valley, CA 94941

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-92-339

Dear Mr. Sutton:

You have requested written confirmation of telephone advice
provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("Act").1/ Because your letter addresses a
general campaign disclosure issue, we consider it to be a request
for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

If a person solicits and receives a photograph from a
candidate or committee, and then independently makes expenditures
to produce a campaign piece incorporating the photograph, are such
expenditures contributions or independent expenditures?

ANSWER

As long as the person does not communicate with the candidate
or the candidate's agent about how or when the campaign piece will
be produced and disseminated, such expenditures are independent
expenditures.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Sutton, James
Our File No:  I-92-339
Page 2


                                ANALYSIS

     Section 82031 defines independent expenditure as:

     [A]n expenditure made by any person in connection with a
communication which expressly advocates the election or
defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified
measure, or taken as a whole and in context,
unambiguously urges a particular result in an election
but which is not made to or at the behest of the
affected candidate or committee.

     A payment which is "made at the behest" of a candidate or
committee is a "contribution" to the candidate or committee.
(Section 82015.)  A payment is "made at the behest" of a candidate
or committee if it is made:

     ... under the control or at the direction of, in
cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert
with, or at the request or suggestion of a candidate,
controlled committee,...

                                    (Regulation 18215.)

     Whether expenditures to produce a campaign piece are
contributions or independent expenditures depends upon the
interaction between the candidate or his/her agents and the person
soliciting and using the photograph.  The Commission has advised
that when a candidate or his/her agent has no control or
involvement in how or when campaign materials are produced and
disseminated, such expenditures are independent expenditures.
(Davis Advice Letter No. I-90-173, and Bargatelos Advice Letter,
No. I-90-364.3/)  Therefore, as long as a person only solicits a
candidate's photograph and does not otherwise engage in activities
with the candidate or his/her agents that meet the definition of
"made at the behest," such campaign expenditures are independent
expenditures.


__________
3/     Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available
in many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials
and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission
at a cost of 10¢ per page.

Sútton, James
Our File No:  I-92-139
Page 3


     Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

                              Sincerely,

                              Scott Hallabrin
                              Acting General Counsel

                              [signature]

                              By:  Lynda Cassady
                                   Political Reform Consultant
